goal: Bring the daemon's external surface alive: HOT/COLD plugin lifecycle (idle → auto-stop, on-demand restart) plus a basic MCP server over stdio that registers all 22 UCIL tools with stubs returning _meta.not_yet_implemented.
plan_summary: Extend crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs with a HOT/COLD state machine (DISCOVERED → REGISTERED → LOADING → ACTIVE → IDLE → STOPPED → ERROR) keyed off manifest [lifecycle] hot_cold + idle_timeout_minutes (master-plan §14.1/§14.2). Introduce crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs housing a JSON-RPC 2.0 MCP server over tokio stdin/stdout that registers all 22 tools from master-plan §3.2 with their schema/description; unimplemented tool handlers return `{"_meta":{"not_yet_implemented":true}}`. Both modules use real tokio IO, real tokio::time::timeout wrappers, and cover behavior with module-level tests whose nextest path exactly matches the frozen acceptance selectors.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — add HOT/COLD machinery as an additive layer on the existing skeleton: (a) `pub enum PluginState { Discovered, Registered, Loading, Active, Idle, Stopped, Error }` with Display impl; (b) `pub struct PluginRuntime { manifest: PluginManifest, state: PluginState, last_call: Instant, idle_timeout: Duration }` holding per-plugin runtime state; (c) `impl PluginRuntime { pub fn mark_call(&mut self), pub fn tick(&mut self, now: Instant) -> Option<PluginState> }` where tick transitions Active → Idle when now - last_call > idle_timeout, and Idle → Loading on the next mark_call; (d) `impl PluginManager` methods `activate(&mut self, manifest) -> PluginRuntime` (Registered → Loading → Active via real health-check), `run_idle_monitor(&mut self, interval: Duration)` spawning a tokio task that periodically calls tick on each runtime. Gate every .await on child-process IO with tokio::time::timeout using named consts (rust-style.md). Respect the manifest [lifecycle] hot_cold + idle_timeout_minutes fields (add them to PluginManifest / lifecycle sub-table; parse default when absent).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs — the acceptance test `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` MUST be a module-level fn decorated with `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test]` (or `#[test]` + runtime), NOT nested in `mod tests { }`. The frozen selector `plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` will not match `plugin_manager::tests::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` — this was the root cause of WO-0006 rejection. The test must exercise a real end-to-end lifecycle: spawn the `mock-mcp-plugin` binary (already wired in Cargo.toml from WO-0009), health-check it (Active), then simulate idle (advance clock or use a short idle_timeout like 200ms) and assert the state transitions to Idle, then call mark_call and assert it returns to Active after a real re-spawn + health check. Use tempfile + tokio::time::sleep; no mocks of tokio::process or child stdio.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs — NEW module: JSON-RPC 2.0 MCP server skeleton over stdio. Export `pub struct McpServer { tools: Vec<ToolDescriptor> }`, `pub struct ToolDescriptor { name: &'static str, description: &'static str, input_schema: serde_json::Value }`, a `pub const UCIL_TOOLS: [ToolDescriptor; 22]` or equivalent populated function listing all 22 tools from master-plan §3.2 (row 1-22 of the table: understand_code, find_definition, find_references, search_code, find_similar, get_context_for_edit, get_conventions, get_architecture, trace_dependencies, blast_radius, explain_history, remember, review_changes, check_quality, run_tests, security_scan, lint_code, type_check, refactor, generate_docs, query_database, check_runtime). Each input_schema MUST be a valid JSON-Schema object with CEQP universal parameters (`reason`: string optional, `current_task`: string optional, `files_in_context`: array optional, `token_budget`: integer optional) per master-plan §8.2. Implement `impl McpServer { pub async fn serve<R, W>(&self, reader: R, writer: W) -> Result<(), McpError>` reading line-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 from reader, dispatching `initialize`, `tools/list` (return the 22 descriptors), and `tools/call` (dispatch to a stub that responds `{"_meta":{"not_yet_implemented":true}}` envelope for every tool). All awaits on IO wrapped with tokio::time::timeout and named const budgets.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs — the acceptance test `test_all_22_tools_registered` MUST be a module-level fn (same rule as above; frozen selector `server::test_all_22_tools_registered`). Test body: construct an McpServer, write a single JSON-RPC `tools/list` request into an in-memory duplex pair (tokio::io::duplex), read the response, parse `result.tools`, assert the array has length 22 AND contains each expected name from §3.2 AND every tool's input_schema contains the 4 CEQP properties. Also exercise one tools/call with name=understand_code and assert the response body carries `_meta.not_yet_implemented == true`. No mocks of serde_json or tokio::io.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod server;` and re-export `McpServer, ToolDescriptor, McpError, UCIL_TOOLS` (or the equivalent accessor). Extend the plugin_manager re-exports with the new `PluginState, PluginRuntime` types. Keep lib.rs logic-free (re-exports only, per rust-style.md).

crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — add whatever deps the server needs if not already in workspace (likely just `serde_json` — already present). Do NOT add new top-level dependencies without checking workspace Cargo.toml first.
PluginError / McpError enums use thiserror (rust-style.md); anyhow allowed only in the binary entry point, not in library code.
scope_out: Wiring Serena / ast-grep / Probe / any real plugin through the server — tool handlers stay stubbed. Real dispatch is Phase 2+ (G1/G2 fusion).
CEQP reason parsing / bonus context compilation — schema only, no runtime enrichment (that is P2 G5).
MCP Elicitation protocol — schema stub only; no elicitation round-trip (P3).
HTTP transport — stdio only in Phase 1 (master-plan §18 Week 3 item 5).
Plugin restart backoff / circuit breakers / max-restart counters — Phase 2 Week 6 (§18 Phase 2 Week 6 item 2). Implement just idle-timeout + on-demand restart.
Progressive startup / first-query latency budget — that is P1-W3-F08, a separate WO.
Crash recovery / checkpoint.json — that is P1-W3-F09, a separate WO.
File watcher integration — that is P1-W3-F02/F03, a separate WO.
Daemon process lifecycle / main.rs wiring — that is P1-W3-F01 already in flight on WO-0008; do NOT touch main.rs in this WO.
Any edit to ucil-build/feature-list.json, ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md, tests/fixtures/**, or scripts/gate/**.
Any edit to crates/ucil-core/** or crates/ucil-treesitter/** — this WO is daemon-only.
acceptance: cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle --no-fail-fast — exactly 1 test resolves and passes
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_all_22_tools_registered --no-fail-fast — exactly 1 test resolves and passes
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager:: --no-fail-fast — prior WO-0009 tests still pass (no regression on P1-W3-F05)
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon session_manager:: --no-fail-fast — prior session_manager tests still pass (no regression on P1-W2-F05)
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings — clean (pedantic + nursery preserved per rust-style.md)
Every tool name in the UCIL_TOOLS/descriptors array matches exactly one row name in master-plan §3.2 (case-sensitive, snake_case).
Every tool's input_schema carries the 4 CEQP optional properties: reason, current_task, files_in_context, token_budget.
tools/call on any unimplemented tool returns a result whose top-level JSON object contains `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true`.
HOT/COLD state machine: after idle_timeout elapses with no mark_call, a runtime's state transitions to Idle; a subsequent mark_call transitions through Loading → Active via real health-check.
grep -RInE 'todo!\(|unimplemented!\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError' crates/ucil-daemon/src returns no matches.
grep -RInE '#\[ignore\]|\.skip\(|xfail|it\.skip' crates/ucil-daemon/src crates/ucil-daemon/tests returns no matches.
grep -RIn 'std::process::Command' crates/ucil-daemon/src returns no matches (async paths use tokio::process::Command per rust-style.md).
acceptance_criteria: cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle --no-fail-fast — 1 passing test
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon server::test_all_22_tools_registered --no-fail-fast — 1 passing test
cargo nextest run --workspace --no-fail-fast — all prior green tests remain green
grep -c 'mod tests' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs is 0 — acceptance test must NOT live under a nested `mod tests { }` (selector path compatibility, per DEC-0005 / WO-0007 lesson)

grep -n '#\[cfg(test)\]' crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs for the HOT/COLD test shows the decorator attached to a module-level `fn test_hot_cold_lifecycle` (peer to the existing `mod tests { }`, not inside it).
jq -e '.features[] | select(.id=="P1-W3-F06" or .id=="P1-W3-F07")' ucil-build/feature-list.json — unchanged (frozen oracle).
context_refs: master-plan:§3.2 Complete tool list (22 tool rows — names + purposes)
master-plan:§8.2 Universal parameters on every tool (CEQP: reason, current_task, files_in_context, token_budget)
master-plan:§10.2 Start-up sequence (item 8 — MCP server begins accepting queries)
master-plan:§14.1 Plugin manifest schema (plugin / transport / resources / lifecycle tables — hot_cold, idle_timeout_minutes defaults)
master-plan:§14.2 Plugin lifecycle (DISCOVERED → REGISTERED → LOADING → ACTIVE → IDLE → STOPPED → ERROR + HOT/COLD spec)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 3 item 4 (plugin manager HOT/COLD) and item 5 (basic MCP server with 22 tools, stubs ok)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror in libs; tokio::process::Command in async paths; tokio::time::timeout on every IO await; no unwrap/expect outside cfg(test); module layout)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (conventional commits, Phase/Feature/Work-order footer, ~50 line soft ceiling, no --amend after push)
phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (phase invariants — stdio MCP, JSON-RPC 2.0, stubs return _meta.not_yet_implemented:true per invariant #9)
decision:DEC-0001-WO-0002-large-commits (single-new-module-file commits accepted as one coherent unit)
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits (extends DEC-0001 to daemon crate modules — applies to the new server.rs)
decision:DEC-0007-remove-cargo-mutants-per-wo-gate (no per-WO mutation-gate run; reality-check.sh still binds)
escalation:ucil-build/escalations/20260415-1856-wo-WO-0006-attempts-exhausted.md (test-selector mismatch root cause — tests must be at module level to match `module::fn_name` selectors)
work-order:ucil-build/work-orders/0007-fix-storage-test-selector.json (precedent fix — flatten `mod tests { }` to module-level for selector match)
work-order:ucil-build/work-orders/0009-salsa-and-plugin-manager.json (existing plugin_manager.rs + mock-mcp-plugin binary — extend, do NOT rewrite)
lessons_applied: WO-0006 rejection lesson: tests whose selector is `module::fn_name` (no trailing `::`) must be placed at module level, NOT inside `mod tests { }` — both `plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` and `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` use this pattern, so the WO explicitly forbids the nested-module wrapping.

DEC-0001/DEC-0005 precedent: the new `server.rs` is a single coherent module (types + impl + module-level test). Permitted to land as one commit ~300-500 lines if splitting would break compilation (e.g., the 22 ToolDescriptor const array references types from the same file).
DEC-0007: no cargo-mutants run is part of this WO's gate. Reality-check.sh stash-mutation remains in force; executor should make sure stashing either module's code causes the acceptance test to fail.
flow_note: Executor creates worktree at feat/WO-0010-plugin-lifecycle-and-mcp-server, implements incrementally, commits+pushes every ~50-line logical chunk (per commit-style.md), writes `ucil-build/work-orders/0010-ready-for-review.md` on local-green. Critic then inspects. Verifier flips P1-W3-F06 and P1-W3-F07 via scripts/flip-feature.sh in a fresh session.
